source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure that Alpha clusters are not used for production workloads

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure that Alpha clusters are not used for production workloads
input_row.description: Alpha clusters are not covered by an SLA and are not production-ready.
input_row.rationale: Alpha clusters are designed for early adopters to experiment with workloads that take advantage of new features before those features are production-ready. They have all Kubernetes API features enabled, but are not covered by the GKE SLA, do not receive security updates, have node auto-upgrade and node auto-repair disabled, and cannot be upgraded. They are also automatically deleted after 30 days. Impact: Users and workloads will not be able to take advantage of features included within Alpha clusters.
input_row.audit: The audit script for this recommendation utilizes 3 variables: $CLUSTER_NAME $COMPUTE_ZONE Please set these parameters on the system where you will be executing your gcloud audit script or command. Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. If a cluster appears under the 'Kubernetes alpha clusters' heading, it is an Alpha cluster. Using Command Line Run the command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME \ --zone $COMPUTE-ZONE \ --format json | jq '.enableKubernetesAlpha' The output of the above command will return true if it is an Alpha cluster.
input_row.remediation: Alpha features cannot be disabled. To remediate, a new cluster must be created. Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/ 2. Click CREATE CLUSTER, and choose "SWITCH TO STANDARD CLUSTER" in the upper right corner of the screen. 3. Under Features in the the CLUSTER section, "Enable Kubernetes alpha features in this cluster" will not be available by default and to use Kubernetes alpha features in this cluster, first disable release channels. Note: It will only be available if the cluster is created with a Static version for the Control plane version, along with both Automatically upgrade nodes to the next available version and Enable auto-repair being checked under the Node pool details for each node. 4. Configure the other settings as required and click CREATE. Using Command Line: Upon creating a new cluster gcloud container clusters create [CLUSTER_NAME] \ --zone [COMPUTE_ZONE] Do not use the --enable-kubernetes-alpha argument. Default Value: By default, Kubernetes Alpha features are disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/alpha-clusters
input_row.impact: Users and workloads will not be able to take advantage of features included within Alpha clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The control is aimed at ensuring that Alpha clusters, which are not suitable for production workloads due to the lack of SLA and security updates, are not used in production environments.
- The control can be checked using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command-line tool as it provides a specific command to verify the status of the Kubernetes Alpha features on clusters.
- The rationale and audit sections both point towards the use of `gcloud` commands to ascertain if a cluster is configured as an Alpha cluster. Specifically, the `gcloud container clusters describe` command is used to determine the `enableKubernetesAlpha` property.
- Since the audit mentions specific `gcloud` commands that can be executed to verify the property (`enableKubernetesAlpha`), this is indicative of a configuration that is exposed via GCP APIs, suitable for automated checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the `gcloud container clusters describe` command to retrieve details of the GKE clusters.
- **Check:** Execute `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json | jq '.enableKubernetesAlpha'`.
- **Validation:** Check the output to verify if the `enableKubernetesAlpha` property is set to `true`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** If `enableKubernetesAlpha` is `false`, confirming that the alpha features are disabled.
  - **Fail:** If `enableKubernetesAlpha` is `true`, indicating that the cluster is using alpha features.
- **Scope:** Enumerate all GKE clusters within the project across available regions and zones.
- **Purpose:** Ensure no clusters with alpha features enabled are used for production to avoid potential stability and security risks that come without SLA coverage.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be fully assessed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) CLI.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Coverage:** The `gcloud container clusters describe` command exposes the `enableKubernetesAlpha` field, which is the authoritative indicator for this control. This property is available via both the CLI and the underlying GKE REST API (`projects.locations.clusters.get`).
- **Other Automation Options:** In addition to `gcloud`, the GKE API can be called directly (REST/Client Libraries), and GCP Config Validator or Security Command Center custom modules could also be leveraged for continuous compliance.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8/10

- **Automation Details:** The described approach is technically correct for identifying whether `enableKubernetesAlpha` is set to `true` or `false`.
- **gcloud Command:** `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json | jq '.enableKubernetesAlpha'` is valid, but only for a single cluster in a single zone.
- **Enumerating Clusters:** The assessment mentions "enumerate all GKE clusters," but does not provide the explicit enumeration step. To be complete, you must first list all clusters across all regions/zones using `gcloud container clusters list --format=json`, then iterate over them.
- **Program Name:** `gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled` is descriptive, but could be more specific (see Improvements).
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Correctly described.
- **Scope:** The scope is correct, but the implementation detail for multi-region/multi-zone enumeration is missing.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Enumerate All Clusters:**  
  - **Missing Step:** The assessment should explicitly state the need to enumerate all clusters across all regions/zones:
    ```sh
    gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,location)"
    ```
    Then, for each cluster:
    ```sh
    gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --region $REGION --format="value(enableKubernetesAlpha)"
    ```
    or
    ```sh
    gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $ZONE --format="value(enableKubernetesAlpha)"
    ```
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**  
  - The same check can be performed using the GKE REST API:  
    `GET https://container.googleapis.com/v1/projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[LOCATION]/clusters/[CLUSTER_NAME]`
    and inspecting the `enableKubernetesAlpha` field.
- **GCP Config Validator:**  
  - For continuous compliance, consider using [Forseti Config Validator](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/config-validator) with a policy that checks this field.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - To be more precise and consistent with best practices:  
    `gcp_gke_cluster_enable_kubernetes_alpha_disabled`
    or  
    `gcp_gke_cluster_no_alpha_features`
    This makes it clear the check is on the `enableKubernetesAlpha` property.
- **Production Tagging:**  
  - The control is about "production workloads." If clusters are tagged/labeled as "production," the script should filter accordingly. Otherwise, the check applies to all clusters, not just production ones.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The control is well understood, the GCP API/CLI provides clear visibility, and the assessment is technically sound with minor omissions.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

- **Modify** the automation details to explicitly include enumeration of all clusters across all regions/zones.
- **Update** the program name to `gcp_gke_cluster_enable_kubernetes_alpha_disabled` for clarity and convention alignment.
- **Optional:** If "production" is defined by labels or naming conventions, include logic to filter only production clusters.
- **Otherwise:** The assessment is fundamentally correct and can be kept with these improvements.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Verdict      | Notes                                                                                      |
|---------------------|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE        | Automated is correct; GCP CLI/API supports this check.                                     |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10         | Correct, but missing enumeration of all clusters and could improve program naming.          |
| Improvements        | See above    | Add enumeration, clarify program name, optionally filter for "production" clusters.         |
| Confidence          | HIGH         | GCP API/CLI fully supports this; control is clear.                                         |
| Final Recommendation| MODIFY       | Add enumeration, update program name, optionally clarify "production" scope.                |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the presence of Alpha clusters can be programmatically determined using the GCP API and CLI, specifically by checking the `enableKubernetesAlpha` property on GKE clusters. The audit steps in the control explicitly reference the use of `gcloud` commands, and the property is exposed via both the CLI and the GKE REST API. This makes the control fully automatable across all clusters in a project.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) clusters
- API/CLI Method:  
  - List clusters: `gcloud container clusters list --format=json`  
  - Describe cluster: `gcloud container clusters describe <CLUSTER_NAME> --zone <ZONE> --format=json`  
  - Or via REST: `projects.locations.clusters.get`
- Properties to Check: `enableKubernetesAlpha` (boolean)
- Pass Criteria: All clusters have `enableKubernetesAlpha` set to `false`
- Fail Criteria: Any cluster has `enableKubernetesAlpha` set to `true` (indicating an Alpha cluster is present)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, but the second opinion noted the need to enumerate all clusters, not just check a single one. My final answer incorporates this completeness by specifying enumeration of all clusters in the project.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
